info.title: OpenFang Daemon API
info.description: REST API for the OpenFang Agent Operating System daemon.

get.summary: GET /.well-known/agent.json — A2A Agent Card for the default agent.
GET /a2a/agents — List all A2A agent cards.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/send — Submit a task to an agent via A2A.
post.description:
Async-from-creation: the task is inserted as `Working` and returned
immediately. A background tokio task drives the agent loop and either
`complete`s or `fail`s the task in the store. Callers poll
`GET /a2a/tasks/{id}` until the status leaves `Working`.

Routing: if any active agent's name contains `"demiurg"` (case-insensitive),
the task is routed there. Otherwise falls back to the first registered
agent (legacy behavior).

Artifact contract: when the agent's reply contains lines of the form
`<artifact path="..." mime="..."/>`, those files are read from the agent's
workspace, base64-encoded, and packed into the task's `artifacts[]` as
`A2aPart::File`. The remaining prose becomes `A2aPart::Text`.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/sendSubscribe — Submit a task and stream updates over SSE.
post.description:
Same request shape as `a2a_send_task` but the response is an SSE stream
of `TaskStatusUpdate` events: one frame per text delta, tool-use, tool
result, and a terminal frame (`final: true`) carrying the assembled
assistant message and token usage. The task is also written into
`kernel.a2a_task_store` so concurrent `GET /a2a/tasks/{id}` polling and
the existing `POST /a2a/tasks/{id}/cancel` route keep working.

Tool calls are surfaced as `A2aPart::Data` parts with mime
`application/vnd.openfang.tool+json` (see `A2A_TOOL_MIME`).

get.summary: GET /a2a/tasks/{id} — Get task status from the task store.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/{id}/cancel — Cancel a tracked task.
post.description:
Flips the task store to `Cancelled` AND aborts the in-flight tokio task
(if any). Aborting drops the future tree, which propagates through every
`.await` inside `send_message_with_session` → `run_agent_loop` → tool
calls — so demiurg's nested `agent_send` / `hand_activate` calls also
stop. `complete`/`fail` no-op when the status is already `Cancelled`,
preventing a finished-just-before-abort race from overwriting the
terminal state.

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/agents — List discovered external A2A agents.

post.summary: POST /api/a2a/discover — Discover a new external A2A agent by URL.
POST /api/a2a/send — Send a task to an external A2A agent.

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/tasks/{id}/status — Get task status from an external A2A agent.
GET /api/a2a/tasks/{tid}/artifacts/{aid} — Download an artifact's bytes.
get.description:
Auth: Bearer or X-API-Key (default `/api/*` middleware applies).
Lifetime: artifact is downloadable while its parent task is in the
`A2aTaskStore`; once the task is evicted (FIFO at capacity), this
endpoint returns 404.

Path-traversal is prevented at artifact construction time
(`extract_a2a_artifacts` canonicalises the path under the agent's
workspace root and refuses anything that escapes); this handler
re-checks the canonicalised path before reading.

get.summary: GET /api/agents — List all agents.

post.summary: POST /api/agents — Spawn a new agent.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id — Get a single agent's detailed info.

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id} — Partial update of agent fields (name, description, model, system_prompt).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/clone — Clone an agent with its workspace files.

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/config — Hot-update agent name, description, system prompt, and identity.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/context — Detailed context window analysis.
GET /api/agents/:id/deliveries — List recent delivery receipts for an agent.
GET /api/agents/{id}/files — List workspace identity files.
GET /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Read a workspace identity file.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Write a workspace identity file.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/agents/{id}/history — Clear ALL conversation history for an agent.

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/identity — Update an agent's visual identity.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/mcp_servers — Get an agent's MCP server assignment info.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/mcp_servers — Update an agent's MCP server allowlist.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/:id/message — Send a message to an agent.
POST /api/agents/:id/message/stream — SSE streaming response.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/:id/mode — Change an agent's operational mode.
PUT /api/agents/{id}/model — Switch an agent's model.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/restart — Restart a crashed/stuck agent.
post.description:
Cancels any active task, resets agent state to Running, and updates last_active.
Returns the agent's new state.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/session — Get agent session (conversation history).
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `include_system` — when `true`, system-role messages are included in the
  response (intended for debugging only). Defaults to `false` so the
  internal system prompt is never leaked into the Web UI conversation
  history (issue #935).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/session/compact — Trigger LLM session compaction.
POST /api/agents/{id}/session/reset — Reset an agent's session.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/sessions — List all sessions for an agent.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions — Create a new session for an agent.

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/by-label/:label — Find session by label (scoped to agent).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/switch — Switch to an existing session.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/skills — Get an agent's skill assignment info.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/skills — Update an agent's skill allowlist.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/stop — Cancel an agent's current LLM run.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/taint-policy — Get an agent's taint/PII policy.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/taint-policy — Update an agent's taint/PII policy.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/tools — Get an agent's tool allowlist/blocklist.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/tools — Update an agent's tool allowlist/blocklist.
PUT /api/agents/:id — Update an agent (currently: re-set manifest fields).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/upload — Upload a file attachment.
post.description:
Accepts multipart/form-data. The client must include a file field with:
- `Content-Type` field (e.g., `image/png`, `text/plain`, `application/pdf`)
- `filename` attribute (original filename)

get.summary: GET /api/approvals — List pending and recent approval requests.
get.description:
Transforms field names to match the dashboard template expectations:
`action_summary` → `action`, `agent_id` → `agent_name`, `requested_at` → `created_at`.

post.summary: POST /api/approvals/{id}/approve — Approve a pending request.
POST /api/approvals/{id}/reject — Reject a pending request.

get.summary: GET /api/audit/recent — Get recent audit log entries.
GET /api/audit/verify — Verify the audit chain integrity.
GET /api/auth/check — Check current authentication state.

post.summary: POST /api/auth/login — Authenticate with username/password, returns session token.
POST /api/auth/logout — Clear the session cookie.

get.summary: GET /api/bindings — List all agent bindings.

post.summary: POST /api/bindings — Add a new agent binding.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/bindings/:index — Remove a binding by index.

get.summary: GET /api/budget — Current budget status (limits, spend, % used).

put.summary: PUT /api/budget — Update global budget limits (in-memory only, not persisted to config.toml).

get.summary: GET /api/budget/agents — Per-agent cost ranking (top spenders).
GET /api/budget/agents/{id} — Per-agent budget/quota status.

put.summary: PUT /api/budget/agents/{id} — Update per-agent budget limits at runtime.

get.summary: GET /api/channels — List all 40 channel adapters with status and field metadata.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/reload — Manually trigger a channel hot-reload from disk config.
POST /api/channels/whatsapp/qr/start — Start a WhatsApp Web QR login session.
post.description:
If a WhatsApp Web gateway is available (e.g. a Baileys-based bridge process),
this proxies the request and returns a base64 QR code data URL. If no gateway
is running, it returns instructions to set one up.

get.summary: GET /api/channels/whatsapp/qr/status — Poll for QR scan completion.
get.description:
After calling `/qr/start`, the frontend polls this to check if the user
has scanned the QR code and the WhatsApp Web session is connected.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/{name}/configure — Save channel secrets + config fields.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/channels/{name}/configure — Remove channel secrets + config section.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/{name}/test — Connectivity check + optional live test message.
post.description:
Accepts an optional JSON body with `channel_id` (for Discord/Slack) or `chat_id`
(for Telegram). When provided, sends a real test message to verify the bot can
post to that channel.

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/browse — Browse ClawHub skills by sort order.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `sort` — sort order: "trending", "downloads", "stars", "updated", "rating" (default: "trending")
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)
- `cursor` — pagination cursor from previous response

post.summary: POST /api/clawhub/install — Install a skill from ClawHub.
post.description:
Runs the full security pipeline: SHA256 verification, format detection,
manifest security scan, prompt injection scan, and binary dependency check.

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/search — Search ClawHub skills using vector/semantic search.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `q` — search query (required)
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/skill/{slug} — Get detailed info about a ClawHub skill.
GET /api/clawhub/skill/{slug}/code — Fetch the source code (SKILL.md) of a ClawHub skill.

GET /api/commands?surface=web|cli|channel|all — List slash commands from the
unified command registry, filtered by surface.
get.description:
Query params:
  - `surface`: one of `web` (default), `cli`, `channel`, `all`.

Returns:
```json
{
  "surface": "web",
  "commands": [
    {
      "name": "new",
      "aliases": ["reset"],
      "description": "Reset session (clear history)",
      "category": "session",
      "requires_agent": true
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

Unknown surface values return 400.

get.summary: GET /api/comms/events — Return recent inter-agent communication events.
get.description:
Sources from both the event bus (for lifecycle events with full context)
and the audit log (for message/spawn/kill events that are always captured).

get.summary: GET /api/comms/events/stream — SSE stream of inter-agent communication events.
get.description: Polls the audit log every 500ms for new inter-agent events.

post.summary: POST /api/comms/send — Send a message from one agent to another.
POST /api/comms/task — Post a task to the agent task queue.

get.summary: GET /api/comms/topology — Build agent topology graph from registry.
GET /api/config — Get kernel configuration (secrets redacted).

post.summary: POST /api/config/reload — Reload configuration from disk and apply hot-reloadable changes.
post.description:
Reads the config file, diffs against current config, validates the new config,
and applies hot-reloadable actions (approval policy, cron limits, etc.).
Returns the reload plan showing what changed and what was applied.

get.summary: GET /api/config/schema — Return a simplified JSON description of the config structure.

post.summary: POST /api/config/set — Set a single config value and persist to config.toml.
post.description:
Accepts JSON `{ "path": "section.key", "value": "..." }`.
Writes the value to the TOML config file and triggers a reload.

get.summary: GET /api/cron/jobs — List all cron jobs, optionally filtered by agent_id.

post.summary: POST /api/cron/jobs — Create a new cron job.

put.summary: PUT /api/cron/jobs/{id}/enable — Enable or disable a cron job.

post.summary: POST /api/cron/jobs/{id}/run — Trigger a cron job immediately.
post.description:
Returns `{"status": "triggered", "job_id": "..."}` and spawns the execution
in the background.  The job's status can be polled via
`GET /api/cron/jobs/{id}/status`.

get.summary: GET /api/cron/jobs/{id}/status — Get status of a specific cron job.
GET /api/evolve/agent — Get the analyzer agent ID.
GET /api/evolve/analyses — List execution analyses (paginated).
GET /api/evolve/analyses/{id} — Get a single analysis by ID.

post.summary: POST /api/evolve/analyze/{session_id} — Analyze a specific session.

get.summary:
GET /api/evolve/batch-apply/preview — Dry-run dedup over current pending
suggestions. Computes the grouping in-memory; performs NO database writes.
Use to preview what the next scheduled batch apply will do.
get.description:
Stores the resulting JSON in `state.batch_apply_snapshot.last_preview` so a
page reload restores the panel.

post.summary: POST /api/evolve/batch-apply/run — Run dedup + execute pipeline now.
post.description:
Same path as the `EvolveBatchApply` cron job. Acquires the evolver
exec lock for the duration so manual `/execute` and the batch run cannot
race. Returns the human-readable summary on success.

Writes `state.batch_apply_snapshot` before and after the call so the
dashboard can show a persistent spinner during the run and the resulting
summary across page reloads.

get.summary:
GET /api/evolve/batch-apply/status — Server-side snapshot of the last
preview or run triggered through this API. Drives the dashboard spinner
and lets the last-preview / last-summary panels survive page reloads.
get.description:
Note: cron-triggered batch applies do not currently write this snapshot
(kernel can't reach API-layer state without a cyclic dep). The cron
scheduler's own run history is the source of truth for those.

get.summary: GET /api/evolve/config — Get the current evolve configuration.

put.summary: PUT /api/evolve/config — Update evolve configuration.

get.summary:
GET /api/evolve/cost — Month-to-date USD spend on analyzer + evolver
agents, plus the configured cap (if any).

post.summary: POST /api/evolve/execute — Execute a single evolution suggestion.
POST /api/evolve/execute-all — Enqueue all suggestions from an analysis.

get.summary:
GET /api/evolve/execute/status — Snapshot of the execute queue for reload
recovery. Returns the most recent snapshot regardless of `running`.

GET /api/evolve/execute/stream — SSE stream of `EvolveExecuteEvent`s
published by the background worker. Each subscriber gets a fresh
receiver from the broadcast channel; missed events (subscribed mid-run)
are surfaced via the `status` endpoint instead.

post.summary: POST /api/evolve/run — Analyze all unanalyzed sessions.

get.summary: GET /api/evolve/run/status — Current snapshot of the most recent evolve batch.
get.description:
The dashboard calls this on page mount; if `running` is true it starts
polling so progress survives reloads of `/api/evolve/run/stream`.

get.summary: GET /api/evolve/run/stream — Same batch as POST /api/evolve/run, streamed as SSE.
get.description:
Emits one `started` event, one `item` event per session processed (with
`index`, `total`, `session_id`, `agent_id`, `status`), and a final
`completed` event. Consumed by the Evolution tab to show realtime progress.

get.summary: GET /api/evolve/skills — List skill records with optional active-only filter.
GET /api/evolve/skills/:id — Get a single skill record by ID.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/evolve/skills/:id — Delete an evolved skill record and its files.

post.summary: POST /api/evolve/skills/:id/rollback — Rollback to parent skill version.

get.summary: GET /api/evolve/stats — Get aggregate evolution statistics.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/evolve/suggestion — Delete a single evolution suggestion.

post.summary: POST /api/evolve/trigger/metrics — Manually trigger skill metric check.

get.summary: GET /api/files/download?root=<n>&path=<rel> — Stream file as attachment.
GET /api/files/list?root=<n>&path=<rel> — Directory listing.
GET /api/files/read?root=<n>&path=<rel> — Inline file content (text only).
GET /api/files/roots — Enumerate the two browseable roots.
GET /api/hands — List all hand definitions (marketplace).
GET /api/hands/active — List active hand instances.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/install — Install a hand from TOML content.

get.summary: GET /api/hands/instances/{id}/browser — Get live browser state for a hand instance.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/instances/{id}/pause — Pause a hand instance.
POST /api/hands/instances/{id}/resume — Resume a paused hand instance.

get.summary: GET /api/hands/instances/{id}/stats — Get dashboard stats for a hand instance.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/upsert — Install or update a hand definition.
post.description:
Like `install_hand` but overwrites an existing definition with the same ID.
Active instances are NOT automatically restarted — deactivate + reactivate
to pick up the new definition.

get.summary: GET /api/hands/{hand_id} — Get a single hand definition with requirements check.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/{hand_id}/activate — Activate a hand (spawns agent).
POST /api/hands/{hand_id}/check-deps — Re-check dependency status for a hand.
POST /api/hands/{hand_id}/install-deps — Auto-install missing dependencies for a hand.

get.summary: GET /api/hands/{hand_id}/settings — Get settings schema and current values for a hand.

put.summary: PUT /api/hands/{hand_id}/settings — Update settings for a hand instance.

get.summary:
GET /api/health — Minimal liveness probe (public, no auth required).
Returns only status and version to prevent information leakage.
Use GET /api/health/detail for full diagnostics (requires auth).
GET /api/health/detail — Full health diagnostics (requires auth).
GET /api/integrations — List installed integrations with status.

post.summary: POST /api/integrations/add — Install an integration.

get.summary: GET /api/integrations/available — List all available templates.
GET /api/integrations/health — Health status for all integrations.

post.summary: POST /api/integrations/reload — Hot-reload integration configs and reconnect MCP.
POST /api/integrations/:id/reconnect — Reconnect an MCP server.

get.summary: GET /api/logs/stream — SSE endpoint for real-time audit log streaming.
get.description:
Streams new audit entries as Server-Sent Events. Accepts optional query
parameters for filtering:
  - `level`  — filter by classified level (info, warn, error)
  - `filter` — text substring filter across action/detail/agent_id
  - `token`  — auth token (for EventSource clients that cannot set headers)

A heartbeat ping is sent every 15 seconds to keep the connection alive.
The endpoint polls the audit log every second and sends only new entries
(tracked by sequence number). On first connect, existing entries are sent
as a backfill so the client has immediate context.

get.summary: GET /api/marketplace/search — Search the FangHub marketplace.
GET /api/mcp/servers — List configured MCP servers and their tools.
GET /api/memory/agents/:id/kv — List KV pairs for an agent.
get.description:
Note: memory_store tool writes to a shared namespace, so we read from that
same namespace regardless of which agent ID is in the URL.

get.summary: GET /api/memory/agents/:id/kv/:key — Get a specific KV value.

put.summary: PUT /api/memory/agents/:id/kv/:key — Set a KV value.

get.summary: GET /api/metrics — Prometheus text-format metrics.
get.description:
Returns counters and gauges for monitoring OpenFang in production:
- `openfang_agents_active` — number of active agents
- `openfang_uptime_seconds` — seconds since daemon started
- `openfang_tokens_total` — total tokens consumed (per agent)
- `openfang_tool_calls_total` — total tool calls (per agent)
- `openfang_panics_total` — supervisor panic count
- `openfang_restarts_total` — supervisor restart count

post.summary: POST /api/migrate — Run migration from another agent framework.

get.summary: GET /api/migrate/detect — Auto-detect OpenClaw installation.

post.summary: POST /api/migrate/scan — Scan a specific directory for OpenClaw workspace.

get.summary: GET /api/models — List all models in the catalog.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `provider` — filter by provider (e.g. `?provider=anthropic`)
- `tier` — filter by tier (e.g. `?tier=smart`)
- `available` — only show models from configured providers (`?available=true`)

get.summary: GET /api/models/aliases — List all alias-to-model mappings.

post.summary: POST /api/models/custom — Add a custom model to the catalog.
post.description:
Persists to `~/.openfang/custom_models.json` and makes the model immediately
available for agent assignment.

get.summary: GET /api/models/{id} — Get a single model by ID or alias.

put.summary: PUT /api/models/{id} — Upsert per-model values as a Custom-tier shadow.
put.description:
Accepts a sparse JSON patch — any field omitted is filled from the model's
current resolved entry (so `find_model` semantics are honoured: builtin →
local → previous Custom override). Persists to `~/.openfang/custom_models.json`.

Use `DELETE /api/models/custom/{id}` to remove the override (Reset action).

get.summary: GET /api/network/status — OFP network status summary.

post.summary: POST /api/pairing/complete — Complete pairing with token + device info.

get.summary: GET /api/pairing/devices — List paired devices.

post.summary: POST /api/pairing/notify — Push a notification to all paired devices.
POST /api/pairing/request — Create a new pairing request (returns token + QR URI).

get.summary: GET /api/peers — List known OFP peers.
GET /api/profiles — List all tool profiles and their tool lists.
GET /api/providers — List all providers with auth status.
get.description:
For local providers (ollama, vllm, lmstudio), also probes reachability and
discovers available models via their health endpoints.

Probes run **concurrently** and results are **cached for 60 seconds** so the
endpoint responds instantly on repeated dashboard loads even when local
providers are unreachable (fixes #474).

get.summary: GET /api/providers/github-copilot/oauth/poll/{poll_id}
get.description:
Poll the status of a GitHub device flow.
Returns `pending`, `complete`, `expired`, `denied`, or `error`.
On `complete`, saves the token to secrets.env and sets GITHUB_TOKEN.

post.summary: POST /api/providers/github-copilot/oauth/start
post.description:
Initiates a GitHub device flow for Copilot authentication.
Returns a user code and verification URI that the user visits in their browser.

post.summary: POST /api/providers/{name}/key — Save an API key for a provider.
post.description:
SECURITY: Writes to `~/.openfang/secrets.env`, sets env var in process,
and refreshes auth detection. Key is zeroized after use.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/providers/{name}/key — Remove an API key for a provider.

post.summary: POST /api/providers/{name}/test — Test a provider's connectivity.

put.summary: PUT /api/providers/{name}/url — Set a custom base URL for a provider.

get.summary: GET /api/schedules — List all scheduled jobs from the cron scheduler.

post.summary: POST /api/schedules — Create a new scheduled job in the cron scheduler.

put.summary: PUT /api/schedules/:id — Update a scheduled job.
put.description:
Supports toggling `enabled`. Other fields are not mutable without a
delete+recreate (the underlying cron scheduler does not expose in-place
edits); those are accepted but ignored with a note in the response.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/schedules/:id — Remove a scheduled job from the cron scheduler.

get.summary:
GET /api/schedules/{id}/delivery-log — Return the last N per-target
delivery results for a schedule.
get.description:
Delivery results are not yet persisted across runs, so this endpoint
returns an empty list for jobs that exist. A future change can back this
with a ring buffer populated by `cron_fan_out_targets`. The endpoint is
wired up now so the dashboard can call it without a placeholder.

post.summary: POST /api/schedules/:id/run — Manually run a scheduled job now.

get.summary: GET /api/security — Security feature status for the dashboard.
GET /api/sessions — List all sessions with metadata.

put.summary: PUT /api/sessions/:id/label — Set a session label.

post.summary: POST /api/shutdown — Graceful shutdown.

get.summary: GET /api/skills — List installed skills.

post.summary: POST /api/skills/create — Create a local prompt-only skill.
POST /api/skills/install — Install a skill from FangHub (GitHub).
POST /api/skills/reload — Hot-reload the skill registry from disk.
post.description:
Called by the CLI after `openfang skill install` to notify the running
daemon that new skill files were added to the skills directory (#752).

post.summary: POST /api/skills/uninstall — Uninstall a skill.

get.summary: GET /api/skills/{id}/config — Return declared + resolved per-skill config.

put.summary: PUT /api/skills/{id}/config — Write user overrides for a skill's config.
put.description:
The body is `{"values": {"<var_name>": "<value>"}}`. Only declared
variables are accepted; unknown keys return 400. After the config.toml is
atomically rewritten, the kernel's skill registry is reloaded so the
change takes effect immediately.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/skills/{id}/config/{var_name} — Remove a single user override.
delete.description:
After removal, the variable falls back to env or default on the next
prompt build. If removing the override would leave a `required` variable
with no resolvable value, the request fails with 409 Conflict so the
caller is never tricked into a broken state.

get.summary: GET /api/skills/{id}/detail — Manifest summary, SKILL.md text, and file list.
get.description:
Used by the Skills tab to render a click-through detail modal for installed
skills. Bundled skills (path sentinel `<bundled>`) are materialized to disk
on first view so the file list always reflects on-disk content.

get.summary: GET /api/skills/{id}/file?path=<relpath> — Single file contents from a skill directory.
get.description:
Enforces path-traversal safety: rejects `..`, absolute paths, null bytes;
canonicalizes both sides and requires the target to remain under the skill
directory. Files >2 MiB are refused. Binary files return `binary: true`
with a null `content` so the UI can show a placeholder instead of garbage.

get.summary: GET /api/status — Kernel status.
GET /api/templates — List available agent templates.
GET /api/templates/:name — Get template details.
GET /api/tools — List all tool definitions (built-in + MCP).
GET /api/triggers — List all triggers (optionally filter by ?agent_id=...).

post.summary: POST /api/triggers — Register a new event trigger.

put.summary: PUT /api/triggers/:id — Update a trigger (enable/disable toggle).

get.summary: GET /api/uploads/{file_id} — Serve an uploaded file.
GET /api/usage — Get per-agent usage statistics.
GET /api/usage/by-model — Get usage grouped by model.
GET /api/usage/daily — Get daily usage breakdown for the last 7 days.
GET /api/usage/summary — Get overall usage summary from UsageStore.
GET /api/version — Build & version info.

post.summary: POST /api/wizard/plan — generate a setup plan from a natural language intent.
POST /api/wizard/spawn — generate a plan and immediately spawn the agent.

get.summary: GET /api/workflows — List all workflows.

post.summary: POST /api/workflows — Register a new workflow.

get.summary: GET /api/workflows/:id — Get a single workflow by ID.

put.summary: PUT /api/workflows/:id — Update a workflow definition.

post.summary: POST /api/workflows/:id/run — Execute a workflow.

get.summary: GET /api/workflows/:id/runs — List runs for a workflow.

post.summary: POST /hooks/agent — Run an isolated agent turn via webhook.
post.description:
Sends a message directly to the specified agent and returns the response.
This enables external systems (CI/CD, Slack, etc.) to trigger agent work.

post.summary: POST /hooks/wake — Inject a system event via webhook trigger.
post.description:
Publishes a custom event through the kernel's event system, which can
trigger proactive agents that subscribe to the event type.

post.summary: POST /mcp — Handle MCP JSON-RPC requests over HTTP.
post.description:
Exposes the same MCP protocol normally served via stdio, allowing
external MCP clients to connect over HTTP instead.

post.summary: POST /v1/chat/completions

get.summary: GET /v1/models — List available agents as OpenAI model objects.

instance_name.description:
Optional unique instance label. Allows multiple instances of the same
hand to coexist as long as each name is distinct.

autonomous_tick_enabled.description:
Opt-out of the autonomous tick loop. `None` ⇒ use default (true).
Has no effect on Reactive hands.

AgentBinding.description: Agent binding — routes specific channel/account/peer patterns to agents.

match_rule.description: Match criteria (all specified fields must match).

AgentHookPayload.description: Payload for POST /hooks/agent — run an isolated agent turn.

message.description: Message to send to the agent (max 16384 chars).

agent.description: Target agent (by name or ID). None = default agent.

deliver.description: Whether to deliver response to a channel.

AgentIntent.description: The extracted intent from a user's natural language description.

task.description: What the agent should do (summarized task).

scheduled.description: Whether it runs on a schedule.

AgentMode.description: Permission-based operational mode for an agent.

AgentUpdateRequest.description: Request to update an agent's manifest.

AttachmentRef.description: A file attachment reference (from a prior upload).

BindingMatchRule.description:
Match rule for agent bindings. All specified (non-None) fields must match.

`#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` is intentional: a typo like `channnel_id` or
`chan_id` would otherwise be silently dropped, producing a wide-open binding
that matches every message. Failing loudly at config load is the safer default.

account_id.description: Specific account/bot ID within the channel.

channel_id.description:
Channel/conversation ID — the per-channel routing dimension.
On Discord this is the channel/thread ID; on Slack it is the conversation
ID (`C…`/`D…`/`G…`); on Telegram it is the chat ID; on IRC it is the
channel name. Bridges populate this from the message's channel/conversation
identifier so bindings can route by room independent of which user posted.

roles.description: Role-based routing (user must have at least one).

reasoning_effort.description:
OpenAI/DeepSeek-style reasoning effort: `"low" | "medium" | "high"`.
Any present value enables streaming reasoning over SSE; absence keeps
the kernel default (no override).

ClawHubInstallRequest.description: Request to install a skill from ClawHub.

CloneAgentRequest.description: Request body for cloning an agent.

CreateApprovalRequest.description:
POST /api/approvals — Create a manual approval request (for external systems).

Note: Most approval requests are created automatically by the tool_runner
when an agent invokes a tool that requires approval. This endpoint exists
for external integrations that need to inject approval gates.

FallbackModel.description: A fallback model entry in a chain.

MessageRequest.description: Request to send a message to an agent.

sender_id.description: Sender identity (e.g. WhatsApp phone number, Telegram user ID).

client_msg_id.description:
Browser-supplied dedupe id stamped on the persisted user turn so the
frontend can match its server-echoed message back to the local bubble.

MigrateScanRequest.description: Request to scan a directory for migration.

PatchAgentConfigRequest.description: Request body for patching agent config (name, description, prompt, identity, model).

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name for the API key.

key_required.description: Whether an API key is required (false for local providers).

model_count.description: Number of models from this provider in the catalog.

SetAgentFileRequest.description: Request body for writing a workspace identity file.

SetModeRequest.description: Request to change an agent's operational mode.

values.description: New per-variable values. Keys must match declared variable names.

SkillInstallRequest.description: Request to install a skill from the marketplace.

SpawnRequest.description: Request to spawn an agent from a TOML manifest string or a template name.

manifest_toml.description: Agent manifest as TOML string (optional if `template` is provided).

template.description:
Template name from `~/.openfang/agents/{template}/agent.toml`.
When provided and `manifest_toml` is empty, the template is loaded automatically.

signed_manifest.description:
Optional Ed25519 signed manifest envelope (JSON).
When present, the signature is verified before spawning.

UpdateIdentityRequest.description: Request body for updating agent visual identity.

WakeMode.description: Wake mode for system event injection.

WakePayload.description: Payload for POST /hooks/wake — inject a system event.

tags.name: migrate
tags.description: Data migration from other agent frameworks
